name: pulls
context: Create, list, review, and merge pull requests via the GitHub API and gh CLI
info: This skill covers the full pull request lifecycle: creating PRs from feature branches or forks, listing and filtering PRs by state and labels, requesting and submitting code reviews, checking CI status from [[github/actions]], and merging via merge commit, squash, or rebase. All operations use the GitHub REST API via @octokit/rest or the gh CLI. Agents use this skill to propose code changes, participate in code review, and land changes into protected branches. PRs can reference [[github/issues]] with closing keywords. See [[github/auth]] for authentication, [[github/repos]] for branch protection and merge requirements, and [[github/rate-limits]] for quota management.

sections.title: Creating pull requests
sections.body: Use `octokit.pulls.create()` or `gh pr create` with title, body, head (source branch), and base (target branch). Set `draft: true` for work-in-progress PRs. Use closing keywords in the body (`Fixes #42`) to auto-close linked [[github/issues]] on merge. See [[github/pulls/create]] for full details.

sections.title: Listing and filtering
sections.body: Use `octokit.pulls.list()` or `gh pr list` to list PRs by state (open/closed/all), sort order, labels, assignees, and head/base branches. For cross-repo search, use `octokit.search.issuesAndPullRequests()` with `is:pr` qualifier. See [[github/pulls/list]] for full details and [[github/pagination]] for traversing results.

sections.title: Review and approval
sections.body: Request reviews with `octokit.pulls.requestReviewers()` or `gh pr review`. Submit reviews with events: APPROVE, REQUEST_CHANGES, or COMMENT. Add inline comments on specific lines. Branch protection in [[github/repos]] can require approvals before merge. See [[github/pulls/review]] for full details.

sections.title: Merge strategies
sections.body: Three merge methods: `merge` (preserves history), `squash` (single commit), `rebase` (linear history). Configure allowed methods per-repo via [[github/repos/settings]]. Use `gh pr merge --squash --delete-branch` for the most common workflow. See [[github/pulls/merge]] for full details.
